streams.description: Critical path — auth → book → operational loop → POS → reports → cutover
Separable ML stream from Wave 6; can slip without blocking MVP (DEC-011)

waves.name: Infrastructure & Docker scaffold
waves.hitl_reason: First Azure deploy needs owner to provision subscription + DNS + ACR credentials (TASK-007, 009, 010 marked PARTIAL for creds)
waves.notes: Monorepo + Dockerfiles per service + docker-compose + IaC + CI/CD + 10 ADRs + feature flags module. Wave 1 exit = a green `docker-compose up` locally AND a deployed hello-world to staging Container Apps.

waves.name: Schema + Conversions + Audit
waves.notes: Property-tested conversions module (AD-4); schema with row-level audit triggers (AD-5); restaurant_id lint rule; seeded portion utensils.

waves.name: Auth + RBAC
waves.notes: JWT-only API + refresh cookie (AD-6); argon2; role guard; PWA login. TEST tasks (026–028) block IMPLEMENT tasks within the wave.

waves.name: Ingredients + Suppliers + Settings
waves.notes: First user-facing modules. English-only PWA (v1.6). Tests precede implementation per TDD rule.

waves.name: Recipes + Station views + Migration parsers
waves.hitl_reason: Plan gate advisory #5 — migration dry-run checkpoint before running staging against real owner data. Also task-gen split candidate (≥ 20 files).
waves.notes: Largest wave. All 8 migration parsers land; recipes + nested BOM + versioning + station cheat-sheet PDFs.

waves.name: Operational loop start // ML baseline training kickoff
waves.notes: Prep sheet + inventory + deliveries PWA screens shipping. ML stream opens (TASK-071 — Holt-Winters baseline).

waves.name: Orders + Waste + Migration review UI // ML training pipeline
waves.hitl_reason: Migration review UI first production use — owner needs to approve the dedupe/mapping experience before real batches are promoted.
waves.notes: Completes operational loop (order + waste + migration review). ML training pipeline stands up.

waves.name: Aloha + Reports + Dashboard // ML inference + accuracy dashboard
waves.notes: Task-gen advisory: if scope feels tight, accuracy dashboard (part of TASK-078) can slip to Wave 9 as already planned.

waves.name: ML wrap + integration
waves.notes: Forecast UI wired into prep sheet + ordering screens; accuracy dashboard live.

waves.name: Cutover + Hardening
waves.hitl_reason: Owner UAT on migration review screens + DR drill sign-off + prod promotion approval (§15 DoD #7).
waves.notes: Perf, a11y, security, DR drill, OpenAPI, and owner cutover. Exit = live pilot with all 12 DoD items signed.

file_conflicts_resolved.resolution: TASK-018 creates schema in Wave 2; TASK-021 adds audit triggers also in Wave 2 but sequenced after TASK-020 test passes
Serialized by wave — each wave adds new pages in a distinct subdirectory; no cross-task overlap within a wave
046 + 047 in Wave 5 (parsers + staging engine); 061 in Wave 7 (review UI)

hitl_gates.reason: Owner review of migration UI before real batch promotion
Owner UAT + DR drill sign-off + prod cutover

notes: Schedule matches plan §9 wave table (~15 wk critical + 3 wk hardening buffer, within v1.6 16–22 wk envelope). ML stream runs in parallel from Wave 6; can slip cleanly without blocking MVP per DEC-011.
